zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton於1229年出生在英國盧特藍德貝爾頓 在 盧特藍德時，他的父親Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham33歲，母親Isabel Odingsells27歲。他於1255年與Cicely Alicia Lovet在英國盧特藍德貝爾頓 在 盧特藍德結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1298年在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾咸頓 洛維特享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton于1229年出生在英国盧特藍德貝爾頓 在 盧特藍德时，他的父亲Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham33岁，母亲Isabel Odingsells27岁。他于1255年与Cicely Alicia Lovet在英国盧特藍德貝爾頓 在 盧特藍德结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1298年在英国英格兰武斯特斯爾咸頓 洛維特享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Blount
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Midlands): variant of   Blunt  .

variants.name: Blunt
meanings: English: nickname for someone with fair hair or a light complexion, from Old French blund, blond (from Latin blondus) ‘blond, fair, yellow-haired’, used also of complexion.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English laford, louerd, lord ‘lord, master’ (Old English hlāford), perhaps given to a someone who played the part of the lord or master of ceremonies in festival games.
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as a translation of the main element of Gaelic Ó Tighearnaigh (see Tierney ) and Mac Thighearnáin (see McKiernan ).
Americanized form of French Laure, a variant of the personal name Laur 3.

variants.name: Tierney
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, John Patrick, Brigid, Clodagh, Kieran, Michael Sean, Siobhan.

Irish: shortened form of O'Tierney, an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tíghearnaigh ‘descendant of Tighearnach’, a byname meaning ‘lord, master’. The Gaelic name is sometimes ‘translated’ as English Lord . Compare Tiernan .

variants.name: Lords
meanings: English: variant of Lord , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is rare in Britain.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Belton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of various places called Belton, for example in Leicestershire, Lincolnshire, and Suffolk. The first element, bel, is of uncertain origin; the second is Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.
English: occasionally a variant of   Bilton  , a habitational name from Bilton in Warwickshire.
Irish: from the Norman name Weldon , relatively common in Ireland, which has sometimes been Gaelicized as de Bhéalatún and re-Anglicized as Veldon and Belton.

variants.name: Bilton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the places in Northumberland and Yorkshire called Bilton, from an Old English personal name Billa + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’. There is also a Bilton in Warwickshire, of which the first element is probably Old English beolone ‘henbane’ (compare Belton ).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton was born in 1229, in Belton In Rutland, Rutland, England, his father, Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham, was 33 and his mother, Isabel Odingsells, was 27. He married Cicely Alicia Lovet in 1255, in Belton In Rutland, Rutland, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1298, in Hampton Lovett, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton est né en 1229, à Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Angleterre, son père, Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Isabel Odingsells, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Cicely Alicia Lovet en 1255, à Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1298, à Hampton Lovett, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 69 ans.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton è nato il nel 1229 nel Belton In Rutland, Rutland, England, il suo padre, Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Odingsells, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Cicely Alicia Lovet nel 1255, nel Belton In Rutland, Rutland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1298, nel Hampton Lovett, Worcestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Beltonが1229年にイギリスルトランドベルトン イン ルトランドで生まれた時，父Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlinghamは33歳，母Isabel Odingsellsは27歳であった。1255年にイギリスルトランドベルトン イン ルトランドでCicely Alicia Lovetと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1298年にイギリスイギリスウオルセステルシュアイアハンプトン ロベットで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton(이)가 1229년 영국 루틀랜드 벨턴 인 루틀랜드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Odingsells(은)는 27세였다. 그는 1255년 영국 루틀랜드 벨턴 인 루틀랜드에서 Cicely Alicia Lovet(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1298년 영국 잉글랜드 워스스터실르 함프턴 러베트에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton nasceu em 1229, em Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Odingsells, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Cicely Alicia Lovet em 1255, em Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1298, em Hampton Lovett, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton родился в 1229 г. в местности белтон ин рутланд, рутланд, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Isabel Odingsells, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Cicely Alicia Lovet в 1255 г. в местности белтон ин рутланд, рутланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1298 г. в местности хамптон лаветт, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Ralph le Blount - Lord of Belton nació en 1229, en Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert Blount 2nd Baron Saxlingham, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Isabel Odingsells, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Cicely Alicia Lovet en 1255, en Belton In Rutland, Rutland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1298, en Hampton Lovett, Worcestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 69 años.
